obj.content: Which [b] TWO [/b] things did Colin find most satisfying about his bread reuse project?
obj.html_content: Which TWO things did Colin find most satisfying about his bread reuse project?

obj.content: receiving support from local restaurants
obj.html_content: receiving support from local restaurants

obj.content: finding a good way to prevent waste
obj.html_content: finding a good way to prevent waste

obj.content: overcoming problems in a basic process
obj.html_content: overcoming problems in a basic process

obj.content: experimenting with designs and colours
obj.html_content: experimenting with designs and colours

obj.content: learning how to apply 3-D printing
obj.html_content: learning how to apply 3-D printing

sentence.content: Collin: I haven’t seen you for a bit, Marie.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：好久不见，玛丽。

sentence.content: Marie: No. I’ve been busy with my project.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：是的，我一直在忙我的项目。

sentence.content: Collin: You’re making a vegan alternative to eggs, aren’t you? Something that doesn’t use animal products?
sentence.content_zh: 科林：你在制作鸡蛋的素食替代品，是吗？不使用动物产品？

sentence.content: Marie: Yes. I’m using chickpeas. I had two main aims when I first started looking for an alternative to eggs, but actually I’ve found chickpeas have got more advantages.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：是的。我用的是鹰嘴豆。刚开始寻找鸡蛋替代品时，我有两个主要目标，但实际上我发现鹰嘴豆有更多优点。

sentence.content: Collin: Right.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：对。

sentence.content: Marie: But how about your project on reusing waste food – you were looking at bread, weren’t you?
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：那你的废弃食物再利用项目呢？你在用面包做试验，对吧？

sentence.content: Collin: Yes. It’s been hard work, but I’ve enjoyed it. The basic process was quite straightforward – breaking the stale bread down to a paste then reforming it.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：是的。工作很辛苦，但我很享受。基本过程很简单——把不新鲜的面包打成糊状，然后再重新加工。

sentence.content: Marie: But you were using 3-D printing, weren’t you, to make the paste into biscuits?
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：但你是用 3-D 打印技术把糊状物制成饼干的，对吗？

sentence.content: Collin: Yeah, I’d used that before, but in this project, I had time to play around with different patterns for the biscuits and finding how I could add fruit and vegetables to make them a more appetising colour, and I was really pleased with what I managed to produce.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：是的，我以前用过，但在这个项目中，我有时间运用不同图案来制作饼，并寻找如何添加水果和蔬菜，使饼干的颜色更有食欲。我对努力产出的成果非常满意。

sentence.content: Marie: It must’ve been a great feeling to make something appetising out of bits of old bread that would’ve been thrown away otherwise.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：用一些本来会被扔掉的旧面包做成美味佳肴，那种感觉一定很棒。

sentence.content: Collin: It was. And I’m hoping that some of the restaurants in town will be interested in the biscuits. I’m going to send them some samples.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：确实如此。我希望镇上的一些餐馆会对这些饼干感兴趣。我打算给他们寄一些样品。

sentence.content: Marie: I came across something on the internet yesterday that might interest you. It was a company that’s developed touch-sensitive sensors for food labels.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：我昨天在网上看到一些东西，你可能会感兴趣。那是一家为食品标签开发触摸感应器的公司。

sentence.content: Collin: Mmm?
sentence.content_zh: 科林：嗯？

sentence.content: Marie: It’s a special sort of label on the food package. When the label’s smooth, the food is fresh and then when you can feel bumps on the label, that means the food’s gone bad.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：这是食品包装上的一种特殊标签。当标签光滑时，食品就是新鲜的，当你能感觉到标签上有凹凸不平的地方时，就意味着食品变质了。

sentence.content: Marie: It started off as a project to help visually impaired people know whether food was fit to eat or not.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：它最初是一个帮助视障人士了解食物是否适合食用的项目。

sentence.content: Collin: Interesting. So just solid food?
sentence.content_zh: 科林：有意思。那么只是固体食物吗？

sentence.content: Marie: No, things like milk and juice as well. But actually, I thought it might be really good for drug storage in hospitals and pharmacies.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：不，还有牛奶和果汁。但实际上，我觉得它对医院和药房的药品储存可能非常有用。

sentence.content: Collin: Right. And coming back to food, maybe it’d be possible to use it for other things besides freshness. Like how many kilograms a joint of meat is, for example.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：对。说回食品，除了保鲜，也许还可以用来做其他事情。例如，一块肉的重量是多少公斤。

sentence.content: Marie: Yes, there’s all sorts of possibilities.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：是的，有各种各样的可能性。

sentence.content: Collin:I was reading an article about food trends predicting how eating habits might change in the next few years.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：我读过一篇关于食品趋势的文章，预测了未来几年饮食习惯可能发生的变化。

sentence.content: Marie: Oh – things like more focus on local products? That seems so obvious, but the shops are still full of imported foods.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：哦-比如更注重本地产品？这似乎很明显，但商店里仍然充斥着进口食品。

sentence.content: Collin: Yes, they need to be more proactive to address that.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：是的，他们需要更加积极主动地解决这个问题。

sentence.content: Marie: And somehow motivate consumers to change, yes.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：是的，以某种方式促使消费者做出改变。

sentence.content: Collin: One thing everyone’s aware of is the need for a reduction in unnecessary packaging – but just about everything you buy in supermarkets is still covered in plastic. The government needs to do something about it.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：每个人都意识到，需要减少不必要的包装。但你在超市买的几乎所有东西仍然都是塑料包装。政府需要为此做些什么。

sentence.content: Marie: Absolutely. It’s got to change.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：当然。必须做出改变。

sentence.content: Collin: Do you think there’ll be more interest in gluten- and lactose-free food?
sentence.content_zh: 科林：您认为人们会对无麸质和无乳糖食品更感兴趣吗？

sentence.content: Marie: For people with allergies or food intolerances? I don’t know. Lots of people I know  have been buying that type of food for years now.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：对于有过敏症或食物不耐受症的人？我不知道。我认识的很多人多年来一直在购买这类食物。

sentence.content: Collin: Yes, even if they haven’t been diagnosed with an allergy.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：是的，即使他们没有被诊断出过敏。

sentence.content: Marie: That’s right. One thing I’ve noticed is the number of branded products related to celebrity chefs – people watch them cooking on TV and then buy things like spice mixes or frozen foods with the chef’s name on . . .
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：没错。我注意到的一件事是，与名厨有关的品牌产品越来越多-人们在电视上看他们做饭，然后购买印有厨师的名字的调料或冷冻食品。

sentence.content: Marie: I bought something like that  once, but I won’t again.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：我买过一次这样的东西，但以后再也不会买了。

sentence.content: Collin: Yeah – I bought a ready-made spice mix for chicken which was supposed to be used by a chef I’d seen on television, and it didn’t actually taste of anything.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：是的，我买了一种现成的鸡肉混合调料，据说是我在电视上看到的一位厨师用的，但实际上什么味道都没有。

sentence.content: Marie: Mm. Did the article mention ‘ghost kitchens’ used to produce takeaway food?
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：嗯。文章有没有提到用来生产外卖食品的"幽灵厨房"？

sentence.content: Collin: No. What are they?
sentence.content_zh: 科林：没有。这是什么？

sentence.content: Marie: Well, they might have the name of a restaurant, but actually they’re a cooking facility just for delivery meals – the public don’t ever go there.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：嗯，幽灵厨房可能有名字，但实际上它们是一个烹饪设施，只用于准备外卖-公众从不去那里。

sentence.content: Marie: But people aren’t aware of that  – it’s all kept very quiet.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：但人们并不知道-幽灵厨房一般都很安静。

sentence.content: Collin: So people don’t realise the food’s not actually from the restaurant?
sentence.content_zh: 科林：所以人们不知道食物实际上不是来自餐馆？

sentence.content: Marie: Right.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：是的。

sentence.content: Collin: Did you know more and more people are using all sorts of different mushrooms now, to treat different health concerns? Things like heart problems?
sentence.content_zh: 科林：你知道现在越来越多的人使用各种不同的蘑菇来治疗不同的健康问题吗？比如心脏病？

sentence.content: Marie: Hmm. They might betaking a big risk there.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：嗯。他们可能要冒很大的风险。

sentence.content: Collin: Yes, it’s hard to know which varieties are safe to eat. Anyway maybe now . . .
sentence.content_zh: 科林：是的，很难知道哪些品种可以安全食用。不管怎么说，也许现在···

obj.content: Which [b] TWO [/b] ways do the students agree that touch-sensitive sensors for food labels could be developed in future?
obj.html_content: Which TWO ways do the students agree that touch-sensitive sensors for food labels could be developed in future?

obj.content: for use on medical products
obj.html_content: for use on medical products

obj.content: to show that food is no longer fit to eat
obj.html_content: to show that food is no longer fit to eat

obj.content: for use with drinks as well as foods
obj.html_content: for use with drinks as well as foods

obj.content: to provide applications for blind people
obj.html_content: to provide applications for blind people

obj.content: to indicate the weight of certain foods
obj.html_content: to indicate the weight of certain foods

sentence.content: Collin: Yes. It’s been hard work, but I’ve enjoyed it. The basic process was quite straightforward – breaking the stale bread down to a paste then reforming it.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：是的。工作很辛苦，但我很享受。基本过程很简单——把不新鲜的面包打成糊状，然后再重新加工。

sentence.content: Marie: But you were using 3-D printing, weren’t you, to make the paste into biscuits?
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：但你是用 3-D 打印技术把糊状物制成饼干的，对吗？

sentence.content: Collin: Yeah, I’d used that before, but in this project, I had time to play around with different patterns for the biscuits and finding how I could add fruit and vegetables to make them a more appetising colour, and I was really pleased with what I managed to produce.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：是的，我以前用过，但在这个项目中，我有时间运用不同图案来制作饼，并寻找如何添加水果和蔬菜，使饼干的颜色更有食欲。我对努力产出的成果非常满意。

sentence.content: Marie: It must’ve been a great feeling to make something appetising out of bits of old bread that would’ve been thrown away otherwise.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：用一些本来会被扔掉的旧面包做成美味佳肴，那种感觉一定很棒。

sentence.content: Collin: It was. And I’m hoping that some of the restaurants in town will be interested in the biscuits. I’m going to send them some samples.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：确实如此。我希望镇上的一些餐馆会对这些饼干感兴趣。我打算给他们寄一些样品。

sentence.content: Marie: I came across something on the internet yesterday that might interest you. It was a company that’s developed touch-sensitive sensors for food labels.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：我昨天在网上看到一些东西，你可能会感兴趣。那是一家为食品标签开发触摸感应器的公司。

sentence.content: Collin: Mmm?
sentence.content_zh: 科林：嗯？

sentence.content: Marie: It’s a special sort of label on the food package. When the label’s smooth, the food is fresh and then when you can feel bumps on the label, that means the food’s gone bad.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：这是食品包装上的一种特殊标签。当标签光滑时，食品就是新鲜的，当你能感觉到标签上有凹凸不平的地方时，就意味着食品变质了。

sentence.content: Marie: It started off as a project to help visually impaired people know whether food was fit to eat or not.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：它最初是一个帮助视障人士了解食物是否适合食用的项目。

sentence.content: Collin: Interesting. So just solid food?
sentence.content_zh: 科林：有意思。那么只是固体食物吗？

sentence.content: Marie: No, things like milk and juice as well. But actually, I thought it might be really good for drug storage in hospitals and pharmacies.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：不，还有牛奶和果汁。但实际上，我觉得它对医院和药房的药品储存可能非常有用。

sentence.content: Collin: Right. And coming back to food, maybe it’d be possible to use it for other things besides freshness. Like how many kilograms a joint of meat is, for example.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：对。说回食品，除了保鲜，也许还可以用来做其他事情。例如，一块肉的重量是多少公斤。

sentence.content: Marie: Yes, there’s all sorts of possibilities.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：是的，有各种各样的可能性。

sentence.content: Collin:I was reading an article about food trends predicting how eating habits might change in the next few years.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：我读过一篇关于食品趋势的文章，预测了未来几年饮食习惯可能发生的变化。

sentence.content: Marie: Oh – things like more focus on local products? That seems so obvious, but the shops are still full of imported foods.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：哦-比如更注重本地产品？这似乎很明显，但商店里仍然充斥着进口食品。

sentence.content: Collin: Yes, they need to be more proactive to address that.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：是的，他们需要更加积极主动地解决这个问题。

sentence.content: Marie: And somehow motivate consumers to change, yes.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：是的，以某种方式促使消费者做出改变。

sentence.content: Collin: One thing everyone’s aware of is the need for a reduction in unnecessary packaging – but just about everything you buy in supermarkets is still covered in plastic. The government needs to do something about it.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：每个人都意识到，需要减少不必要的包装。但你在超市买的几乎所有东西仍然都是塑料包装。政府需要为此做些什么。

sentence.content: Marie: Absolutely. It’s got to change.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：当然。必须做出改变。

sentence.content: Collin: Do you think there’ll be more interest in gluten- and lactose-free food?
sentence.content_zh: 科林：您认为人们会对无麸质和无乳糖食品更感兴趣吗？

sentence.content: Marie: For people with allergies or food intolerances? I don’t know. Lots of people I know  have been buying that type of food for years now.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：对于有过敏症或食物不耐受症的人？我不知道。我认识的很多人多年来一直在购买这类食物。

sentence.content: Collin: Yes, even if they haven’t been diagnosed with an allergy.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：是的，即使他们没有被诊断出过敏。

sentence.content: Marie: That’s right. One thing I’ve noticed is the number of branded products related to celebrity chefs – people watch them cooking on TV and then buy things like spice mixes or frozen foods with the chef’s name on . . .
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：没错。我注意到的一件事是，与名厨有关的品牌产品越来越多-人们在电视上看他们做饭，然后购买印有厨师的名字的调料或冷冻食品。

sentence.content: Marie: I bought something like that  once, but I won’t again.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：我买过一次这样的东西，但以后再也不会买了。

sentence.content: Collin: Yeah – I bought a ready-made spice mix for chicken which was supposed to be used by a chef I’d seen on television, and it didn’t actually taste of anything.
sentence.content_zh: 科林：是的，我买了一种现成的鸡肉混合调料，据说是我在电视上看到的一位厨师用的，但实际上什么味道都没有。

sentence.content: Marie: Mm. Did the article mention ‘ghost kitchens’ used to produce takeaway food?
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：嗯。文章有没有提到用来生产外卖食品的"幽灵厨房"？

sentence.content: Collin: No. What are they?
sentence.content_zh: 科林：没有。这是什么？

sentence.content: Marie: Well, they might have the name of a restaurant, but actually they’re a cooking facility just for delivery meals – the public don’t ever go there.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：嗯，幽灵厨房可能有名字，但实际上它们是一个烹饪设施，只用于准备外卖-公众从不去那里。

sentence.content: Marie: But people aren’t aware of that  – it’s all kept very quiet.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：但人们并不知道-幽灵厨房一般都很安静。

sentence.content: Collin: So people don’t realise the food’s not actually from the restaurant?
sentence.content_zh: 科林：所以人们不知道食物实际上不是来自餐馆？

sentence.content: Marie: Right.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：是的。

sentence.content: Collin: Did you know more and more people are using all sorts of different mushrooms now, to treat different health concerns? Things like heart problems?
sentence.content_zh: 科林：你知道现在越来越多的人使用各种不同的蘑菇来治疗不同的健康问题吗？比如心脏病？

sentence.content: Marie: Hmm. They might betaking a big risk there.
sentence.content_zh: 玛丽：嗯。他们可能要冒很大的风险。

sentence.content: Collin: Yes, it’s hard to know which varieties are safe to eat. Anyway maybe now . . .
sentence.content_zh: 科林：是的，很难知道哪些品种可以安全食用。不管怎么说，也许现在···

officialExplain.explain: 本题需选出男生Colin对于自己的旧面包回收项目最满意的两个方面。原文首先提到basic process，为C选项的原词重现，考生需了解straightforward的意思是“简单的，不复杂的”，和C选项中overcoming problems表达的含义不符。接着女生提到E选项的3-D printing，男生很快回答I’d used that before，可否定E选项。后文提到patterns，对应D选项的designs，colours则为原词重现。根据最后表明态度的语句“I was really pleased with…”可确定D项为正确答案之一。随后女生提到“It must’ve been a great feeling…”，提示下一个答案可能出现。后文“make something appetising out of…would’ve been thrown away otherwise”为B选项中prevent waste的同义替换，需要考生充分理解句意。之后根据男生肯定的回答“It was.”可确定B选项为另一个正确答案。最后男生提到A选项中的restaurants，但只是希望餐馆能对此项目感兴趣，没有提到receiving support。

本题需要选出两人对于触摸感应式食品标签未来发展趋势一致认同的两方面。考生需要留意future这一时态考点。题干中的touch-sensitive sensors for food labels原词重现。女生首先提到了“It started off…help visually impaired people know whether food was fit to eat or not.”。visually impaired对应D选项的blind，需要考生对此处同义替换和单词发音都比较敏感。同时这里也提到了B选项的内容。但是，原文中的started off和题干中的future不符，因而否定B、D两项。接着男生提问是否仅仅用于solid food，女生回答还有milk和juice，为C选项drinks的下义词替换，但这仍不是对未来的推测。转折词But actually之后出现“I thought it might be really good for drug storage…”，might表示推测可能性，和题干的future一致，drug替换A选项的medical products。接着男生回答right，表达认同，因此可确定A选项为正确答案之一。随后男生还提到“it’d be possible…”，提示下一个未来可能的应用即将出现，kilograms替换E选项的weight。之后女生回答Yes，因此E选项为另一个正确答案。
选项D中的should do more替换为need to be more proactive；encourage替换为motivate。
选项G中的stricter regulations对应原文中the government needs to do something。替换较为抽象，考生读到regulations时，需要联想到政府等设立制度或者法律的机构。
选项C中的“already…widespread”对应原文“have been buying…for years”。widespread替换为lots of people。虽然后文提到even if they haven’t been diagnosed with an allergy，但二人并未对此做法的利弊发表看法，因此不能选择B或者H选项。
选项B中的disappointing在二人语言中均有体现。女生说的but I won’t again和男生所说的it didn’t actually taste of anything均表达了对名厨代言食品的不满。考生需识别这两句话中包含的态度信息。此处请参照“文本解析”第六句和第七句的翻译。
选项F中的 know little about this在原文中有多次替换：女生所说的people aren’t aware of that和it’s all kept very quiet，以及男生在下一句中提到的“people don’t realise…”。
选项H中的dangerous对应原文a big risk，以及下一句中的“it’s hard to know…safe to eat”。
